events.headline: US-Iran war enters second week — Pentagon reports 140 US troops injured in first 10 days
events.summary: The US-Israeli war against Iran (Operation Epic Fury / Roaring Lion) entered its second week with no ceasefire in sight. The Pentagon confirmed 140 US troops injured, 8 severely, in the first 10 days of fighting. Defense Secretary Hegseth vowed the 'most intense day yet' of US strikes. The White Hou

events.headline: Brent crude surges 25% to $116.50/bbl — gold hits $5,195.60 as markets roil
events.summary: Brent crude surged over 25% to $116.50/bbl amid the worsening West Asia conflict, with experts warning of $150/bbl if Hormuz remains blocked. Gold shattered records at $5,195.60/oz. The Dow fell over 800 points and S&P 500 sank despite a brief 13% oil price dip when Trump predicted de-escalation. G7

events.headline: Iran begins laying mines in Strait of Hormuz as US Navy suspends escort operations
events.summary: Iran's IRGC began laying naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz, further blocking the critical oil chokepoint. Simultaneously, the US Navy informed commercial shipping that Hormuz escorts are 'not possible for now,' leaving tankers without military protection. Energy Secretary Chris Wright posted then

events.headline: Volkswagen to slash 50,000 jobs in Germany amid profit decline
events.summary: Volkswagen announced plans to cut 50,000 jobs in Germany, citing sinking profits and competitive pressure from Chinese EV manufacturers. This is the largest single job-cut announcement in German automotive history and reflects structural decline in Europe's largest industrial sector.

events.headline: US Dow falls 800+ points as Iran war jitters persist despite oil dip
events.summary: The Dow Jones fell more than 800 points, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq also sinking as Iran war jitters persisted even as oil prices briefly dipped. The sell-off reflects growing recession fears and the market's view that the conflict will be prolonged.

events.headline: US steps up strikes as Trump offers mixed signals — boots on ground 'not ruled out'
events.summary: The White House confirmed US ground troops in Iran are 'not ruled out' while Defense Secretary Hegseth vowed the 'most intense' military strikes yet against Iran. Trump simultaneously offered contradictory timelines — calling the operation 'very complete' while escalating. Kurdish invasion of Iran r

events.headline: Oil price volatility — 13% drop on Trump de-escalation prediction, then recovery
events.summary: Crude oil prices swung wildly, dropping 13% after Trump predicted Middle East de-escalation, before recovering as fighting continued. The extreme volatility (>20% intraday range) reflects the market's sensitivity to every geopolitical signal from Washington and Tehran.

events.headline: Hezbollah escalates war — Macron vows to protect Lebanon from conflict spillover
events.summary: Hezbollah continued its 'reckless war' from Lebanon against Israel, endangering the country. French President Macron declared France would act to protect Lebanon from escalation in the Iran war. Analysis suggests Hezbollah's actions are pushing Lebanon toward state failure.

events.headline: US Navy sinks IRIS Dena — Sri Lanka evacuates crew of second Iranian vessel
events.summary: The US Navy sank the Iranian naval vessel IRIS Dena. Sri Lanka subsequently evacuated the crew of a second Iranian vessel in its waters, indicating expanding naval operations in the Indian Ocean beyond the Persian Gulf theater.

events.headline: Mexico's El Mencho (CJNG cartel leader) killed by military forces
events.summary: Mexican military forces killed Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes ('El Mencho'), leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), one of the world's most powerful drug trafficking organizations. A Mexican general rejected claims that El Mencho had surrendered, stating he 'had a lot of weaponry' when conf

events.headline: G7 finance ministers signal coordinated SPR release amid oil crisis
events.summary: G7 finance ministers discussed oil market stabilization, signaling a coordinated Strategic Petroleum Reserve release to tamp down surging crude prices. The release would be the largest coordinated SPR drawdown since 2022 and targets the 'fear premium' embedded in energy markets from the Gulf conflic

events.headline: Trump supports Kurdish invasion of Iran — US jets providing air cover
events.summary: President Trump confirmed US jets would support a Kurdish ground invasion of Iran, opening a new dimension to the conflict through proxy forces. Kurdish groups operating from Iraq's Kurdistan region are advancing into Iranian territory with US air support.

events.headline: Tehran under heavy US-Israeli bombing — residents describe 'hearts shaking'
events.summary: Tehran residents described enduring heavy Israeli-US bombing with 'hearts shaking.' The capital city is under sustained aerial assault. Iran's internet has been shut down, exposing civilian risks. The humanitarian situation in Iran is deteriorating rapidly.

events.headline: Ukraine retakes territory as Russia's buffer zone strategy falters
events.summary: Ukraine reportedly retook territory as Russia's buffer zone strategy in border regions faltered. Both sides claimed front-line progress with US-brokered trilateral peace talks on hold. The conflict continues as a frozen/active war while the world's attention pivots to Iran.

events.headline: EU transfers €90 billion to Kyiv bypassing Hungary's veto
events.summary: The EU cleared a mechanism to transfer €90 billion to Ukraine by stepping over Hungary's veto through an enhanced cooperation framework. This is a significant institutional precedent for EU decision-making when a single member state blocks consensus.

events.headline: Companies sue for Trump tariff refunds after SCOTUS ruling — $175B windfall
events.summary: Multiple companies filed lawsuits seeking refunds on Trump tariffs after a Supreme Court ruling reshaped the tariff landscape. The total potential refund pool is estimated at $175 billion. Senate Democrats also introduced a bill to shield small businesses from tariff impacts.

events.headline: Bond market stress — leveraged basis trade starts to unwind
events.summary: A hidden leveraged trade in US Treasury bonds — the 'basis trade' — began unwinding amid market stress from the Iran crisis. This $1+ trillion trade involves hedge funds exploiting small price differences between Treasury futures and cash bonds with high leverage. Its unwind recalls the March 2020 T

events.headline: Kristi Noem fired from DHS — Trump creates 'Shield of the Americas' envoy role
events.summary: President Trump fired DHS Secretary Kristi Noem, reassigning her to a new 'Shield of the Americas' Western Hemisphere security envoy position. The move came amid criticism of immigration policy execution. A combative confirmation hearing for her replacement drew attention. The shake-up signals inter

events.headline: Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai accepts 20-year sentence — will not appeal
events.summary: Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai, founder of Apple Daily, decided not to appeal his conviction and 20-year prison term under the National Security Law. The British citizen's case has been a major point of contention in UK-China relations.

events.headline: China vows to accelerate technological self-reliance and AI push at 'Two Sessions'
events.summary: At the annual 'Two Sessions' parliamentary meetings, China's leadership vowed to accelerate technological self-reliance with a major push into AI. One-person 'claw-powered' AI companies became a hot topic. The declarations reinforce China's decoupling strategy from Western technology dependencies.

events.headline: UK's HMS Dragon crew departs for Gulf deployment amid Iran conflict
events.summary: Tearful relatives of HMS Dragon crew described 'scary and emotional' farewells as the Royal Navy destroyer departed for deployment to the Gulf region amid the Iran conflict. The deployment signals UK military involvement in the crisis.

events.headline: India approves limited easing of Chinese investment curbs after years of friction
events.summary: India approved a limited easing of restrictions on Chinese foreign direct investment, partially reversing curbs imposed after the 2020 Galwan border clash. The move signals cautious diplomatic thaw between the world's two most populous nations, driven partly by India's need for Chinese manufacturing

events.headline: India export cargo worth ₹346 crore to Iran stranded at Kandla and Mundra ports
events.summary: Indian export cargo worth approximately $40 million destined for Iran is stranded at Kandla and Mundra ports due to the conflict. The government confirmed the situation to parliament, highlighting the direct trade disruption from the Gulf war beyond just oil.

events.headline: Petro's leftist coalition tops Colombia Congressional elections
events.summary: President Petro's leftist Pacto Histórico coalition won the most seats in Colombia's congressional elections, strengthening his governing mandate. The result gives Petro greater legislative power to advance his reform agenda including land reform, tax changes, and energy transition policies.

events.headline: US midterm mood favors Democrats as GOP faces Iran war and Noem fallout
events.summary: Multiple polls show the midterm mood favoring Democrats despite party image problems. Georgia voters chose a successor to Marjorie Taylor Greene. Sen. Rand Paul warned Republicans are heading toward 'disastrous' midterms if the Iran war continues. The GOP is in 'course-correction mode' after the Noe

events.headline: US proposes fresh trilateral peace talks on Ukraine — Zelensky confirms
events.summary: The US proposed a fresh round of trilateral peace talks next week involving Washington, Kyiv, and Moscow. Zelensky confirmed the proposal. Talks come as both sides claim battlefield gains and the US is distracted by the Iran war.

events.headline: 5 recession warning signs emerge in US economy amid oil shock
events.summary: Multiple analysts identified 5 warning signs of a potential US recession including surging energy costs, tightening consumer budgets, bond market stress (leveraged basis trade unwinding), falling consumer confidence, and supply chain disruptions from the Gulf conflict.

events.headline: Argentina President Milei woos Wall Street — cites economic turnaround with net energy exports
events.summary: Argentine President Milei courted Wall Street investors, highlighting Argentina's achievement of net energy exports for the first time — particularly significant as the Gulf crisis drives global energy prices higher. Vaca Muerta shale production is boosting export revenues.

events.headline: Ghana parliament revives dangerous anti-LGBT bill
events.summary: Ghana's parliament revived a controversial anti-LGBT bill that could impose severe criminal penalties. The legislation risks triggering sanctions from Western donors and could affect Ghana's access to World Bank and IMF concessional financing.

events.headline: US-Israeli missile strike near Iranian girls' school draws condemnation
events.summary: A US-Israeli missile strike near a girls' school in Iran drew international condemnation. Trump stated the US is 'willing to live with' the report, generating backlash. WHO verified 13 attacks on health sites in Iran. The civilian toll is rising and generating international pressure for ceasefire.
